title: Conditional GO remediation cycle plan
outOfScope: Production code, workflows, tests, release metadata, and installer behavior
Creating or enabling platform branch-protection rules
Opening, approving, merging, or closing external pull requests

sources.reason: User-provided Conditional GO review and unresolved P0/P1 findings.
Defines one Work Item, one branch, one PR and lifecycle closure.
Existing completed release-identity baseline; prevents duplicate scope.
Existing completed compatibility baseline; defines the incremental follow-up.
Existing completed adoption-branch baseline; defines transactional follow-up.
Existing completed lockfile-source baseline; defines regeneration-gate follow-up.

riskAssessment.reason: The plan coordinates eight independent remediation Work Items while preserving the repository's one-active-Work-Item lifecycle. External platform approvals remain explicit human gates.

executionDecision.reason: The user requested a bounded execution plan; implementation and external authority actions are explicitly separated.

preReviewWarnings: Each child Work Item must stop for human confirmation when platform permissions, release publication, or external review evidence is unavailable.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A dated plan file lists all eight incremental remediation Work Items, their dependencies, exact repository areas, acceptance gates, and cycle transitions.
The plan preserves one active Work Item, one dedicated branch, one PR, and lifecycle closure before advancing.
The plan identifies external approval and release-publication steps as human-gated rather than silently automating them.
The plan defines a stop condition and restart condition for every child Work Item.
guidelines: Do not combine independent remediation Work Items on one branch or PR.
Do not claim a Work Item is complete until its required checks, PR merge, and lifecycle closure are evidenced.
Do not infer platform approval, protected-branch state, or release publication from repository-controlled files.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
